channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Lucky endangered quoll survives 600km hitchhike in Qld
item.description: Members of the public spotted the marsupial fleeing the wheel arch of a truck in Brisbane and alerted authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Willcox

media:group.media:description: A northern quoll has been released back into the wild near Rockhampton.

item.title: Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigns amid dam controversy
item.description: The ABC understands Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton resigned on Friday after almost six years in the position.
item.dc:creator: Johanna Marie

media:group.media:description: Sunwater CEO Glenn Stockton has resigned after almost six years in the role.

item.title: 'Reward allies, remove critics': Ex-council CEO under ICAC spotlight
item.description: ICAC is investigating allegations the former CEO of City of Parramatta Council subverted hiring practices to fill job vacancies with friends.
item.dc:creator: Tony Ibrahim

media:group.media:description: ICAC has launched an inquiry into current and former City of Parramatta Council staff.

item.title: Man says drug-smuggling dad's double life led to 'nightmare' charges
item.description: A man accused of helping his father carry out a drug importation operation in Newcastle tells a jury his dad led a double life.
item.dc:creator: Giselle Wakatama

media:group.media:description: James Blee is accused of helping his father attempt to import a large quantity of cocaine into Australia.

item.title: Queensland DNA lab clears backlog of 600 outstanding rape kits
item.description: Forensic Science Queensland was the subject of two commissions of inquiry, over failures in testing and cultural issues which may have led to miscarriages of justice.

media:group.media:description: Forensic Science Queensland has been the subject of two commissions of inquiry and two reports which found a litany of issues with the lab's testing of DNA evidence.

item.title: ADFA cadet charged after hidden camera found in bathroom
item.description: An Australian Defence Force Academy cadet who allegedly used a camera hidden in a soap dispenser to film female cadets in the bathroom has been granted bail in the ACT Magistrates Court.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: Tain Stephens, 20, has been charged with five counts of intimate observations and capturing visual data.

item.title: Appeal court overturns guilty conviction for pilot charged over fatal plane crash
item.description: Leslie Woodall had been flying three tourists from Agnes Water in central Queensland when the engine failed and the plane crashed, killing a woman.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: Jocelyn Spurway, 29, was killed in the crash on a beach in central Queensland.

item.title: Melbourne IT teacher jailed for stabbing school principal
item.description: The court heard IT teacher Kim Ramchen "snapped" and stabbed Keysborough Secondary College principal Aaron Sykes while children were still in class.
item.dc:creator: Harrison Tippet

media:group.media:description: Kim Ramchen has been jailed for 15 months for stabbing Keysborough Secondary College principal Aaron Sykes.

item.title: Hundreds of pregnant sheep reported stolen from WA farm
item.description: A farmer in Western Australia's Great Southern region has lost about $100,000 after someone stole hundreds of his pregnant sheep.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Chounding and Kate Forrester

media:group.media:description: More than 270 sheep have been reported stolen from a Pingrup Farm.

item.title: 'Got no friends left': Jewish teen bullied while playing Minecraft
item.description: The royal commission heard the 15-year-old was subjected to antisemitic slurs while playing the popular video game with students from a Perth school.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: A teenage boy told the royal commission he was subjected to antisemitic slurs while playing Minecraft.

item.title: WA will fail to achieve net-zero by 2050, Woodside-funded report warns
item.description: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.
item.dc:creator: Cason Ho

media:group.media:description: A new report finds that the proposed Browse gas project would help WA's energy security in meeting net-zero.

item.title: Mum whose son died of meningococcal B welcomes free vaccines in Victoria
item.description: The government will fund a $9 million program to vaccinate students against meningococcal B from January 1 next year.

media:group.media:description: Norliah Syer-Peterson has been praised for her advocacy for meningococcal B vaccination after her son, Levi, died.

item.title: Trees razed as North Adelaide golf course redevelopment gets underway
item.description: Workers have begun cutting down trees at the North Adelaide Golf Course as part of a controversial redevelopment at the site.

media:group.media:description: An excavator at the North Adelaide golf course as trees begin to be removed for its redevelopment.

item.title: Why are people trying to raid Scientology buildings around the world?
item.description: Over the weekend, Brisbane's Church of Scientology became the latest target of a viral trend that began in the United States just a few months ago — the Scientology speed run.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Clarke and Tara Cassidy

media:group.media:description: Police say 200 people turned up to a viral speed running event in the Brisbane CBD over the weekend.

item.title: 'We can be a voice for women': Afghan cricketers push for ICC recognition
item.description: Afghanistan's exiled women cricketers are urging the International Cricket Council (ICC) to follow FIFA's lead in recognising them as a legitimate national team.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale and Daniela Intili

media:group.media:description: Afghanistan's women's cricket team bowler Shafiqa Khan now lives as a refugee in Australia.

item.title: Matt Wright back with family after what he says was 'one hell of a fight'
item.description: Outback Wrangler star Matt Wright has served five months in a Darwin prison, revealing he was not released in time for the birth of his third child.
item.dc:creator: Olivana Lathouris

media:group.media:description: In a post shared on social media, Matt Wright says he's returned home but "didn't quite make the birth" of his son Sterling.

item.title: Traffic camera captures 'spectacular' meteor over Tasmania
item.description: Tasmanians across the state reported seeing the bright lights of a meteor last night. Astronomer Martin George says the spectacle was typical of a natural object burning up in the atmosphere.

item.title: India tells citizens to shun gold, cut fuel use amid energy crisis
item.description: India's prime minister has urged Indians to work from home, avoid travel and stop buying gold, as the Iran War continues to send energy costs soaring.

media:group.media:description: India's Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has told citizens to refrain from buying gold for weddings.

item.title: Media mogul Antony Catalano excused from appearing in court in person
item.description: A judge allowed the newspaper executive to appear in court via video link from his lawyer's office as he faces charges of assault, false imprisonment and making threats to kill.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Antony Catalano is facing numerous charges relating to an incident in March.

item.title: Investigations underway after 82yo woman dies in house fire
item.description: Fire and Rescue NSW removed an unconscious 82-year-old woman from a house fire in Orange and performed CPR, but she died at the scene.
item.dc:creator: Emily Middleton and Lani Oataway

media:group.media:description: The 82-year-old woman was found unconscious inside house on Tynan Street, Orange.

item.title: Woman killed, daughter hospitalised in Mother's Day crash on the Gold Coast
item.description: A 41-year-old woman has died after a horror Mother's Day crash in the Gold Coast hinterland that left two others critically injured.
item.dc:creator: Emily Dobson

media:group.media:description: A 41-year-old Coomera woman has died in a three-vehicle traffic crash at Maudsland.

item.title: A billionaire mined their land without permission. Now judgment day is looming
item.description: Tomorrow will mark a historic day for an outback WA community as the Federal Court decides how much compensation it is due after Andrew Forrest's FMG mined its land without permission.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes and Mietta Adams

media:group.media:description: Andrew Forrest of Fortescue Metals Group (left) and Michael Woodley of the Yindjibarndi Aboriginal Corporation.

item.title: Pregnant paramedic punched in stomach after saving patient's life
item.description: A pregnant paramedic has been assaulted in Melbourne less than a fortnight after the stabbing of a colleague, the union says.

media:group.media:description: Ambulance Victoria has condemned an assault on a pregnant paramedic by a patient.

item.title: Herbert wins maiden LIV title, secures US Open spot
item.description: Australian Lucas Herbert is off to the US Open after scoring a nerve-shredding four-shot victory at LIV Golf Virginia in Washington DC.

media:group.media:description: Lucas Herbert claimed his maiden LIV Golf tournament success.

item.title: 'The Black Elvis' to perform at the Sydney Opera House
item.description: Uncle Roger Knox, aka the Black Elvis, will perform a collection of his hit songs and a few yarns from his life at the Sydney Opera House, sharing the stage with his son and two grandsons.
item.dc:creator: Kris Flanders

media:group.media:description: The Gomeroi man is known as the Koori King of Country.
